visible_structures: basal ganglia (caudate nucleus, putamen, globus pallidus)
anatomy.hemisphere_symmetry: symmetric — the visible structures appear symmetric

tissue_findings.gray_matter: Appears normal in signal intensity and morphology.
tissue_findings.white_matter: Appears normal in signal intensity and morphology. No focal lesions are identified.
tissue_findings.ventricles: The lateral ventricles are mildly enlarged, particularly the temporal horns. The third ventricle is normal in size. The fourth ventricle is not well visualized in this slice.
tissue_findings.sulci_gyri: The sulci are mildly widened, consistent with age-related changes.
tissue_findings.basal_ganglia: The caudate nucleus, putamen, and globus pallidus are visible and appear normal.
tissue_findings.thalamus: The thalamus is visible and appears normal.
tissue_findings.cerebellum: The cerebellum is visible and appears normal.
tissue_findings.brainstem: The midbrain, pons, and medulla are visible and appear normal.

pathology_flags.notes: Mild enlargement of the lateral ventricles is noted, which may be related to age-associated volume loss.

global_impression: This sagittal T1-weighted MRI demonstrates mild generalized cerebral atrophy with mild widening of the sulci and mild enlargement of the lateral ventricles. No acute intracranial pathology is identified.
